title: Selectors, Cardinality, Unknown Values, and Typed Extraction · Diagnostic Repair Bay
summary: A preserved TypeScript crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, Fetch, stream, Cheerio, selector, frontier, Promise, browser, axe, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first invalid transition before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in runtime-validated extraction contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Define selector scope, expected cardinality, fallback order, exclusions, and source location before extracting.
Represent absent, empty, malformed, duplicate, conflicting, and admitted values as distinct outcomes.
Keep attributes, text, JSON-LD, configuration, and plugin output unknown until validators produce domain values.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Selector Contract
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript team handles case 8329 during a hostile redirect, stream, and checkpoint drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove selector cardinality, missingness, unknown runtime validation, field normalization, and drift alarms; URL, robots, body, parser, frontier, privacy, accessibility, and report budgets stay explicit; then change one owner permission, seed, URL, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, media, or encoding decision and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define selector scope, expected cardinality, fallback order, exclusions, and source location before extracting.

Inspect the Selector Contract trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Selector Contract.

lines.label: risk

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define selector scope, expected cardinality, fallback order, exclusions, and source location before extracting. Artifact under review: runtime-validated extraction contract.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Selector Contract, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Selector Contract record says runtime-validated extraction contract looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Selector Contract.

For inspect the failure record, fetch one convenient page, omit owner authorization, source and emitted revision, URL and robots policy, runtime admission, stream and Promise ownership, Cheerio and browser differences, accessibility states, changed fixtures, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as TypeScript crawler proof. This does not satisfy Selector Contract for runtime-validated extraction contract.
